name: M365 IMAP Migration Batch
summary: Create and optionally start an Exchange Online IMAP migration batch.
description: Creates or reuses an Exchange Online IMAP migration endpoint, uploads CSVData to New-MigrationBatch, and optionally starts the batch. This workflow orchestrates Microsoft 365 migration services; it is not a local IMAP copy engine.
outputs: Writes an HTML batch report plus summary, per-user status, JSON diagnostics, and text notes artifacts.

fields.label: Tenant admin UPN
fields.helpText: Optional sign-in hint for Exchange Online device-code authentication.

fields.label: Migration endpoint name
fields.helpText: Existing endpoint to reuse or new endpoint to create for the source IMAP system.

fields.label: IMAP server
fields.helpText: Source IMAP hostname reachable by Microsoft 365 migration services.

fields.label: Security
fields.helpText: Use Ssl for SSL/TLS on 993, Tls for STARTTLS where supported, or None only for trusted lab scenarios.

fields.label: CSV path inside backend container
fields.helpText: Mounted local path containing EmailAddress, UserName, Password, and optional UserRoot. Leave blank to use M365_TOOLBOX_IMAP_MIGRATION_CSV_PATH. Do not paste passwords into the browser.

fields.label: Skip IMAP certificate validation (high risk)
fields.helpText: High risk. Use only for temporary testing against a known source host with a certificate issue.

fields.label: Migration batch name
fields.helpText: Must be unique in Exchange Online. Existing batches are not overwritten.

fields.label: Target delivery domain
fields.helpText: Optional. Use only when your IMAP migration design requires a target delivery domain.

fields.label: Notification email addresses
fields.helpText: Optional comma-separated notification recipients for Exchange Online migration updates.

fields.label: Start batch after creation
fields.helpText: If disabled, the batch is created but must be started later.

fields.label: Auto-complete batch
fields.helpText: Passes AutoComplete to New-MigrationBatch so Exchange Online completes the batch automatically when eligible.

fields.label: Tenant ID or Domain
fields.helpText: Optional. If provided, device-code sign-in is scoped to this tenant.

notes: Approval is required because this workflow creates Exchange Online migration objects and may start mailbox synchronization.
IMAP migration moves mail only, not contacts, calendars, tasks, mailbox rules, or permissions.
Mailbox passwords must come from a mounted CSV file path or M365_TOOLBOX_IMAP_MIGRATION_CSV_PATH, not browser input.
Large mailboxes and message size limits still follow Exchange Online migration service limits.
prerequisites: PowerShell 7 and backend Microsoft Graph modules available in the execution container
Microsoft 365 admin sign-in by device code when prompted
Exchange Online permissions for the chosen account

examples.title: Default tenant-wide run
examples.description: Use the default field values and authenticate when the device-code prompt appears.

examples.title: Scoped admin review
examples.description: Provide a tenant id or domain to scope authentication to the expected Microsoft 365 tenant.
